key_insight: PPI-GD reduces gradient oracle calls by interpolating gradients from equidistant data-domain samples, yielding better oracle complexity than standard GD variants when the loss is sufficiently smooth.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical path to faster training for large ERM problems when data-domain smoothness can be exploited, especially in high-sample regimes with moderately low data dimension.
method: Query first-order oracle at equidistant points in the data domain
Build piecewise tensor-product polynomial interpolants of gradient samples on patches
Use the interpolated gradient inside an inexact gradient descent scheme
evidence_signal: The paper provides oracle-complexity analysis for strongly convex and non-convex losses and shows improved complexity in key regimes under a polylogarithmic bound on data-space dimension.
watch_item: Performance depends on sufficient smoothness and a manageable data-domain dimension, so interpolation error and patch construction may limit gains in practice.
paper_title: On the Oracle Complexity of Interpolation-Based Gradient Descent
